page: Amazon Web Services m9g.2xlarge is a general-purpose virtual machine instance powered by the Nitro hypervisor. It features 8 dedicated physical cores and 8 vCPUs based on the 64-bit ARMv9 architecture, utilizing the AWS Neoverse-V3 processor running at 2.8 GHz. The CPU configuration includes a 64 KB L1 data cache, 2 MB L2 cache, and 48 MB L3 cache, with hardware support for AES cryptography. It is equipped with 32.0 GB of system memory, providing a balanced 4.0 GB of RAM per core. The instance does not include local storage or dedicated GPU hardware, and it offers a baseline network bandwidth of 4 Gbps.

In benchmark evaluations, the m9g.2xlarge demonstrates strong memory performance, achieving a top-tier PassMark memory score and high memory bandwidth, with read-only small block speeds reaching 1081.51 GB/sec. Single-threaded gzip compression and bzip3 decompression also perform in the top tier, at 36.45 GB/sec and 16.84 GB/sec respectively. However, CPU encryption performance is relatively weak, as shown by a PassMark encryption score of 2363.25. Large-model LLM prompt processing is also a bottleneck, with the medium 7B model processing at a weak 22.43 tokens per second, though text generation for the same model remains average at 15.63 tokens per second.

From a cost-efficiency perspective, the ARM-based architecture provides a highly resource-dense profile for general-purpose workloads, delivering competitive memory bandwidth and standard compute capabilities without the overhead of x86 licensing or hyperthreading. This instance is well-suited for applications such as static web serving, Redis caching, database operations, and lightweight single-threaded compression tasks, but is less optimal for heavy cryptographic processing or large-scale LLM inference.
description: Amazon Web Services m9g.2xlarge is a general-purpose instance featuring 8 dedicated vCPUs on the 64-bit ARMv9 architecture, powered by an AWS Neoverse-V3 processor running at 2.8 GHz. It is equipped with 32.0 GB of RAM, yielding a balanced 4.0 GB per core, but lacks local storage and GPU accelerators. Built on the Nitro hypervisor, the instance delivers 4 Gbps of baseline network bandwidth. Benchmark data highlights top-tier memory performance and single-threaded compression speeds, though it exhibits weaker performance in CPU encryption and medium-model LLM prompt processing. This resource profile offers a cost-efficient balance of memory and compute for general-purpose workloads. It is best suited for static web serving, database operations, Redis caching, and general application hosting.
og_description: Amazon Web Services m9g.2xlarge is an ARMv9-based general-purpose instance featuring 8 vCPUs, 32 GB RAM, and 4 Gbps bandwidth, offering top-tier memory performance for web serving and database workloads.
meta_description: Amazon Web Services m9g.2xlarge is an ARMv9 general-purpose instance with 8 vCPUs and 32 GB RAM, optimized for web serving and database workloads.
tagline: A balanced ARMv9-powered instance delivering exceptional memory bandwidth and efficient compute for diverse general-purpose and web-serving workloads.
bullet_points: 8 dedicated ARMv9 Neoverse-V3 vCPUs running at 2.8 GHz
32.0 GB of system memory with top-tier bandwidth performance
4 Gbps baseline network bandwidth on the Nitro hypervisor
No local storage or GPU hardware included
Excellent fit for static web serving, Redis caching, and database operations
